steps.name: alias-aware philosophy advisory resolver (pure, display-only)
steps.description: Add a pure, total lexicon function that classifies a claim `--object` string against the embedded philosophy seeds for the compose advisory: a canonical known object, an alias of a known philosophy (returning the canonical name), an in-namespace object that is neither, or an out-of-namespace object (not a philosophy — no advisory). Display-only: this NEVER changes any stored/signed bytes. `find()` today matches name/object-id only — the ALIAS arm is genuinely new.
criteria: AC-004.1 (known → canonical; alias → canonical name, marked alias)
AC-004.2 (in-namespace-but-unknown → a distinct 'not a known philosophy' verdict)
out-of-namespace object → a distinct 'not a philosophy, no advisory' verdict

PBT: for every seed, its object_id resolves Canonical(name) and each of its aliases resolves Alias(canonical=name); an unknown in-namespace segment resolves Unknown; an out-of-namespace string resolves NotPhilosophy
steps.implementation_notes: Add a pure ADT + resolver, e.g. `pub enum ObjectAdvisory { Canonical { name: String }, Alias { canonical: String }, UnknownInNamespace, NotPhilosophy }` and `pub fn resolve_object_advisory(object: &str) -> ObjectAdvisory`. Algorithm: if `object` does NOT start with the philosophy object-id prefix (`org.openlore.philosophy.`) → `NotPhilosophy` (reuse/derive the prefix constant already implied by `object_id`). Else take the segment after the prefix and, over `seeds()`: if `normalize(segment) == normalize(seed.name)` → `Canonical { name: seed.name }`; else if any `normalize(alias) == normalize(segment)` for that seed → `Alias { canonical: seed.name }`; else (no seed matches) → `UnknownInNamespace`. Total + offline (embedded seeds only; no store/network). REUSE `seeds()`, `normalize()`, and the prefix used by `object_id()` — do NOT hardcode a second copy of the prefix. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): proptest — property over every seed (object_id → Canonical, each alias → Alias{canonical=name}); an in-namespace random-unknown segment → UnknownInNamespace; an arbitrary non-prefixed string → NotPhilosophy. Keep xtask check-arch green (pure core, 21 members).

phases.name: Advisory line in the claim-add compose preview (cli)

steps.name: render the advisory line in render_compose_preview — display-only, signed bytes byte-unchanged
steps.description: Integrate the advisory into the `claim add` compose preview (`render_compose_preview` in verbs/claim_add.rs): for a philosophy-namespace `--object`, append one advisory line derived from `resolve_object_advisory` — a resolution line for a known/alias object (naming the canonical philosophy, marking alias resolutions), or a NON-BLOCKING 'not a known philosophy — will be signed as-is' warning for an in-namespace unknown. An out-of-namespace object appends NOTHING (preview byte-identical to today). NEVER blocks; NEVER routes resolution into the signed payload — the advisory feeds the PREVIEW STRING ONLY, the signed object bytes remain exactly what the user typed (AC-004.3).
criteria: AC-004.1 (known → resolution advisory; alias → resolves to canonical, marked alias)
AC-004.2 (unknown in-namespace → non-blocking warning; the claim STILL signs unchanged on confirm)
AC-004.3 (byte-parity: signed <cid>.json object == the typed object verbatim, never the canonical rewrite; advisory is display-only)
CA-1 (WS), CA-2 (alias), CA-3 (unknown-still-signs), CA-4 (byte-parity), CA-5 stays GREEN (out-of-namespace → no advisory)
steps.implementation_notes: In `render_compose_preview(claim: &ComposedClaim)` (claim_add.rs ~line 290), after the existing preview body, call `lexicon::resolve_object_advisory(&claim.object)` and append ONE line based on the verdict: Canonical{name} → e.g. `↳ resolves to <name>`; Alias{canonical} → e.g. `↳ resolves to <canonical> (alias)`; UnknownInNamespace → e.g. `⚠ not a known philosophy — will be signed as-is`; NotPhilosophy → append NOTHING (byte-identical preview — CA-5). The three domain substrings the ATs pin (case-insensitive) are `resolves to`, `alias`, `not a known philosophy` — keep them literally present; exact layout/emoji is your choice. CRITICAL (AC-004.3 / D3): the advisory affects ONLY the preview STRING. Do NOT touch `build_unsigned_claim`/the ComposedClaim object/the signed bytes — the object the user typed is signed verbatim. The verb never rejects on an unknown object (non-blocking). No new preview parameter that changes signing. TEST PARADIGM: acceptance CA-1..5 are pre-authored subprocess tests — do NOT rewrite them; any new pure preview-shaping helper gets an example/property unit test. Keep the existing `not as truth` literal + confidence labels intact (no preview regression). check-arch green, 21 members.

validation.reviewer: orchestrator (authored from settled ADR-059 design §5 row 25) + automated quality gate
validation.review_verdict: APPROVED — automated quality gate PASS (decomposition ratio 2 steps / 2 production files = 1.0, CA-1..4 owned by 02-01 + the pure resolver unit-owned by 01-01, valid DAG 01-01 -> 02-01, IDs match NN-NN, criteria <=5, DISTILL linkage present via test_file+scenario_name). Thin advisory slice fully specified by ADR-059 §5 row 25 + US-PV-004 AC-004.1..3; display-only, byte-unchanged signed payload is the load-bearing invariant (CA-4). Alias resolution is a small pure lexicon seam here; the read-time aggregation is slice-26 (OUT).
